ms_lysate.text: DETECTED (GROUP-LEVEL) — 3 unique peptides (OpenProt XP_047300390.1). ATTRIBUTION NOT ESTABLISHED BY MS, AND NOT ESTABLISHABLE: 18 same-stop ORFs in the 763-ORF catalogue CONTAIN N-Myc's entire sequence (ATG-001 at 593 aa, ATG-002 at 575 aa, and 16 near-cognate extensions), so NO peptide of N-Myc can EVER exclude them — this holds whatever the three peptides are. N-Myc's identity rests on ANNOTATION, not on these peptides; annotation is not MS evidence. What the MS establishes is that A PROTEIN FROM THIS STOP GROUP IS TRANSLATED.

ms_lysate.text: DETECTED (GROUP-LEVEL) — 4 unique peptides (OpenProt ENSP00000491476.1). ATTRIBUTION IS IMPOSSIBLE IN PRINCIPLE, PROVEN BY IN-SILICO TRYPTIC DIGEST (dmycn_digest.py; no OpenProt peptide retrieval required). dMYCN's 253 aa = a 52-aa N-terminal leader SHARED VERBATIM with MYCNOT, MUSEP and ORF 11, plus a 201-aa C-terminal block SHARED VERBATIM with N-Myc. 52 + 201 = 253: dMYCN HAS ZERO RESIDUES OF ITS OWN. Only a peptide SPANNING the 52|53 junction could discriminate it; of its 41 MS-detectable tryptic peptides, ZERO are unique. The shortest junction-spanning peptide is 33 aa (AESPPDPLQSAGDDEDDEEEDEEEEIDVVTVEK, residues 41-73) — PAST the 30-aa detectability ceiling — and all six junction peptides are in any case SHARED with NC-EXT-033 and NC-NEST-013/-014/-015. Impossible on TWO INDEPENDENT GROUNDS, robust across windows 7-30 through 7-50 aa and 2 missed cleavages. OpenProt's 4 peptides therefore CANNOT be discriminating peptides: they must lie in the shared leader or the shared C-terminal block. A PROTEIN FROM THIS STOP GROUP IS TRANSLATED; WHICH START PRODUCED IT IS NOT ESTABLISHED.

ms_lysate.text: DETECTED (GROUP-LEVEL) — 7 unique peptides (OpenProt NP_001280162.1/Q9H224). MYCNOT IS THE LIVE POSITIVE CONTROL: it proves the MS ASSAY FIRES at this locus — a short regulatory ORF at MYCN IS detectable by lysate MS — and it is what makes the THREE genuine MS negatives (MUSEP, ATG-015, ATG-022) informative rather than under-powered. IT DOES NOT DISCRIMINATE ITS OWN START: MYCNOT shares its stop with 23 other ORFs in the 763-ORF catalogue, and NC-EXT-035 (CTG, 119 aa) CONTAINS MYCNOT IN ITS ENTIRETY — its 7 peptides cannot exclude it. THE CONTROL'S SCOPE IS THE ASSAY, NOT ATTRIBUTION — INCLUDING ITS OWN. It licenses the claim 'a protein from this stop group is translated'; it licenses NOTHING about which start produced it, and it does NOT extend the SEARCH SPACE.

ms.role: novel ORF — first in the 763 with direct protein evidence

24.source: OpenProt 2.2 IP_083082 (MS-detected, 2 peptides); sequence GENCODE v49 (GRCh38.p14)

ms_lysate.text: DETECTED (GROUP-LEVEL) — THE PEPTIDES DO NOT DISCRIMINATE THE START. OpenProt IP_083082 reports 2 unique peptides (SGVPPPTPR, LSPHSPSLCVPR) at the OpenProt reporting floor, across 3 lysate datasets. THE TWO PEPTIDES ARE CONTAINED IN 6 OF THE 8 sORFs.org-DETECTED REGION-1 MEMBERS, AND IN 16 ORFs ACROSS THE FULL 763-ORF CATALOGUE (2 ATC/ATT extensions LARGER than ATG-016, the 5 ATG starts, and near-cognates down to NC-NEST-092; 2 further ORFs contain one peptide). THE RIBO-SEQ CLUSTER (8) AND THE MS CANDIDATE SET (16) ARE DIFFERENT SETS WITH DIFFERENT DENOMINATORS — records != events, on two axes, with two bases. Any of the 16 would yield exactly these two peptides. The peptides EXCLUDE NC-NEST-128 (contains neither) and cannot be explained by NC-NEST-108 alone (contains only LSPHSPSLCVPR). The other starts are NOT catalogued by OpenProt and were NEVER SEARCHED — NOT-ASSESSED, not ABSENT. A PROTEIN IS TRANSLATED FROM THIS STOP GROUP; WHICH START PRODUCED IT IS NOT ESTABLISHED. Proteome-uniqueness EXCLUDES a canonical-fragment explanation; it CANNOT ATTRIBUTE THE START — the uniqueness search (uniqueness_digest.py, b0514f64) compared against FOUR hard-coded proteins plus UniProt, a database that does NOT contain the nested competitors. evidence_resources.label: MS-lysate (OpenProt 2.2)
evidence_resources.caveat: READ THE TWO LIMITS OF THIS AXIS BEFORE READING ANY CELL. (1) COVERAGE: the MYCNOT 7-peptide control proves the ASSAY FIRES at this locus; it does NOT extend the SEARCH SPACE. OpenProt catalogues only 9 of the 763 ORFs; every ORF without an accession was NEVER IN THE SEARCH SPACE and so was NEVER SEARCHED = NOT-ASSESSED, NOT a negative. The ONLY real MS negatives are the 3 ORFs that ARE catalogued and returned 0 peptides. (2) ATTRIBUTION: NO ORF AT THIS LOCUS IS START-DISCRIMINATED BY LYSATE MS. The locus is a dense nest of same-stop ORFs, and EVERY MS-detected ORF has a same-stop host that contains it in its entirety (N-Myc 18 hosts, MUSEP 5, ATG-016 2, dMYCN 1, MYCNOT 1 — the control included), so no peptide of it can ever exclude that host. Peptide-level START attribution is STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE here — everywhere, not only in Region 1. THE NAMED PROTEINS ARE IDENTIFIED BY ANNOTATION, NOT BY MS. THE HONEST CEILING OF THIS AXIS IS: 'A PROTEIN FROM THIS STOP GROUP IS TRANSLATED' — FOR ALL FOUR DETECTIONS, N-MYC INCLUDED. Proteome-uniqueness EXCLUDES a canonical-fragment explanation; it CANNOT ATTRIBUTE THE START.)

collapse_rule: ONE BAR PER START THE EVIDENCE CAN DISTINGUISH. The rest of a REGION collapses into ONE bar with INTERNAL START TICKS. THE KEY IS THE REGION -- Table B's own cluster assignment -- NOT THE SHARED STOP. A region is a SHARED READ PILE: the thing the evidence cannot resolve into separate starts. A shared stop is a coordinate coincidence, and collapsing on it would MERGE N-Myc WITH dMYCN (8 ORFs share stop 15,946,094). ORFs in no region draw on their own -- that is not a special case; it is what 'the evidence cannot distinguish its start from its siblings' means when it has no siblings. BAR COUNT = EVENT COUNT: one row per event per lane.
start_codon_note: start_codon is EXPLICIT on all 56 ORFs. The 48 original ORFs are ATG -- stated, not defaulted: a field absent because it was never needed is indistinguishable from a field absent because it was lost. The 8 near-cognates (ORFs 49-56) are 5 CTG + 3 GTG. Derived from the coding nt sequence (Table B col AB) and CROSS-VALIDATED against Table B's own source string (col Z, 'start codon XXX').

transcript_provenance.finding: THE VIEWER MIXES THREE ANNOTATION SOURCES AND THE DISPLAY NAMES CONCEAL IT. GENCODE v49 - the annotation the benchmark used - contains only SIX MYCN transcripts (MYCN-201..206). TWO OF THE EIGHT RENDERED ISOFORMS ARE NOT IN IT: MYCN-207 is a GENCODE v50 transcript (ENST00001138178.1), and MYCN-XM is a RefSeq model that is in no GENCODE release at all. A display name is not citable and is not stable across releases; 'MYCN-XM' LOOKS official and is not.
transcript_provenance.version_pinning: Versions are PINNED to GENCODE v49. v50 re-versions 202: ENST00000638417.1 -> ENST00000638417.2, 204: ENST00000885101.1 -> ENST00000885101.2, 205: ENST00000930195.1 -> ENST00000930195.2. The viewer carries the v49 versions because that is the annotation the analysis used. An accession without its version is not an identifier.

annotation_independent.basis: Annotation-independent: these exist as named proteins with accessions, independently of the evidence axes below.
annotation_independent.selection_key: evidence_independent.basis == 'annotation'  (a QUERY on the promoted table, not a curated list)

region1_result.title: The Region-1 result
region1_result.claim: A novel protein IS translated from the Region-1 stop group, out-of-frame within the N-Myc CDS, at 2 proteome-unique peptides (SGVPPPTPR, LSPHSPSLCVPR) across 3 lysate datasets. WHICH OF 8 STARTS produced it is NOT ESTABLISHED.
denominators: The 2 peptides are contained in 6 of the 8 Region-1 members (the Ribo-seq read pile).
They are also contained in 16 ORFs across the full 763-ORF catalogue (the MS candidate set).
DIFFERENT SETS, DIFFERENT DENOMINATORS, DIFFERENT QUESTIONS. A number without its basis is the defect. Any of the candidates would yield exactly these two peptides.
region1_result.why: This IS the result. It is worth more than a tile on any single ORF, because it VISIBLY DECLINES THE STRONGER CLAIM: detection is not attribution.

excluded.note: What is deliberately NOT on this panel, and why. These are not omissions.
items: NO ORF-24 tile. Its state is DETECTED (GROUP-LEVEL). A tile reading 'ORF 24 - protein detected' IS the start claim, in the most prominent position on the site. OpenProt IP_083082 is a CATALOGUE RECORD ID, not an annotation.
NO 'likely real' tier. There is no probability axis in this data. A tier that cannot be derived is an editorial judgement dressed as a finding.
NO ORF 11. Not MS-detected, not annotation-independent. (The selection key excludes it on its own: it is independent only on the basis 'no region'.)
